name: Louredo da Serra Trail
description: Explore the Until Nevogilde circular route, a moderate 9.0-mile (14.5 km) touring bicycle trail with 1118 feet (341 metres) of elevation gai
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Gondalães offer a diverse landscape for exploration. The region features varied terrain, from areas suitable for road cycling to more challenging paths. Natural features like the Pedra do Monte de Marecos provide scenic points of interest and varied topography. Historical sites such as the Torre dos Alcoforados and the Senhor dos Aflitos Chapel add cultural richness to the cycling experience. Best touring cycling routes around Gondalães The most popular touring cycling route is Louredo da Serra Trail, a 9.0 miles (14.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a notable elevation gain of over 1,100 feet. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Memorial of the Hermitage – Quintandona Schist Village loop from Irivo, a difficult 25.7 miles (41.4 km) path. This route includes significant climbs and passes through the historic Quintandona Schist Village. Local touring cyclists also love the Bust of Miguel Sousa Tavares – Bicha Serpe loop from Penafiel, a 19.8 miles (31.9 km) trail leading through areas with cultural landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Gondalães is defined by varied topography, cultural landmarks, and a mix of road and off-road paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Gondalães are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 290 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Gondalães's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Gondalães?
A: Gondalães offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll find varied terrain, from paved roads suitable for road cycling to more challenging paths with significant elevation changes. The region's topography includes hills, such as those leading to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/770851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra do Monte de Marecos&lt;/a&gt;, providing both challenging ascents and rewarding descents.

Q: Are there many difficult touring cycling routes in Gondalães?
A: Yes, Gondalães features a significant number of challenging routes. Out of 54 touring cycling routes, 35 are classified as difficult. These often include substantial climbs and cover longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32142753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Memorial of the Hermitage – Quintandona Schist Village loop from Irivo&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 25.7 miles (41.4 km) with over 2,000 feet of elevation gain.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes in Gondalães offer a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle past the impressive natural monument &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/770851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra do Monte de Marecos&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhor dos Aflitos Chapel&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32142077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhora do Salto Waterfall – River Douro loop from Oleiros&lt;/a&gt; route specifically takes you to a beautiful waterfall and along the River Douro.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Gondalães?
A: While Gondalães offers a variety of routes, most are classified as moderate or difficult, with no routes specifically listed as easy. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380596859/louredo-da-serra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Louredo da Serra Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option at 9.0 miles (14.5 km), which might be suitable for families with older, more experienced cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Gondalães?
A: The touring cycling routes in Gondalães are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the cultural landmarks encountered, and the mix of road and off-road paths that provide diverse riding experiences.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Gondalães are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32142753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Memorial of the Hermitage – Quintandona Schist Village loop from Irivo&lt;/a&gt; and the cultural &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23742269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bust of Miguel Sousa Tavares – Bicha Serpe loop from Penafiel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites can I explore while cycling in Gondalães?
A: Gondalães is rich in history. You can incorporate visits to sites like the imposing 14th-century fortified manor house &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/884025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre dos Alcoforados&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhor dos Aflitos Chapel&lt;/a&gt; into your cycling tours. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19806700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhor dos Aflitos Chapel – Alto do Pico loop from Penafiel&lt;/a&gt; is a route that passes by one of these significant landmarks.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in Gondalães?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes available for exploration in Gondalães, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Is there a route that celebrates the region's cycling heritage?
A: While not a specific route, the town of Sobrado, near Gondalães, is home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5432193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sobrado Volta a Portugal Winners Memorial&lt;/a&gt;. This memorial celebrates the town's impressive achievement of producing four Volta a Portugal cycling race winners, highlighting the region's strong connection to road cycling.

Q: What is a good moderate touring cycling route for someone looking for a shorter ride?
A: For a moderate and relatively shorter ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380596859/louredo-da-serra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Louredo da Serra Trail&lt;/a&gt;. It's 9.0 miles (14.5 km) long and typically takes about 1 hour and 5 minutes to complete, featuring a notable elevation gain of over 1,100 feet.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through traditional villages?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32142753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Memorial of the Hermitage – Quintandona Schist Village loop from Irivo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that takes you through the historic Quintandona Schist Village, offering a glimpse into traditional regional architecture and culture.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Gondalães?
A: While specific seasonal details are not provided, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant weather for cycling in regions like Gondalães, with milder temperatures and less extreme conditions than summer or winter. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Until Nevogilde — circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it covers approximately 14.5 km, it includes about 340 meters of elevation gain, suggesting rolling terrain with some significant ascents. Cyclists with some experience will find it enjoyable, but beginners might find certain sections challenging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain around Nevogilde, in the Lousada area, often features a mix of surfaces. While many routes are predominantly paved and suitable for touring bicycles, some sections might include loose ground. You can expect beautiful countryside, passing through small villages, characteristic of Northern Portugal's rugged mountains and winding roads.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. You'll pass by the Pelourinho de Louredo, a historical monument. You can also enjoy views from Serra de São Tiago and see the historic Romanesque Aqueduct of Nevogilde.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Northern Portugal generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer heat and winter rains. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and lush scenery. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Until Nevogilde — circular route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for this exact route is not available. However, as Nevogilde is a parish within Lousada, you would typically look for parking in or near the village center or at designated public parking areas within the municipality. Consider starting points that offer convenient access and parking.

faqs.question: Can I reach the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options directly to the specific trailhead are not detailed. Lousada, where Nevogilde is located, has regional bus services. It's advisable to research local bus routes connecting to Nevogilde or nearby towns and plan your journey accordingly, keeping in mind that carrying a touring bicycle on public transport might have restrictions.

faqs.question: Is the Until Nevogilde — circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this route are not provided, generally, dogs are allowed on trails in Portugal, especially in rural areas. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock, and to carry waste bags to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access the Until Nevogilde — circular route. It is a public trail, and you can enjoy it freely.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official trails. You will find it overlaps with parts of the Trilho de Louredo da Serra (12% of the route), Caminho dos Paços (4%), and Ponte românica de Nevogilde (4%).

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities along the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through small villages, you may find local cafes or small shops where you can purchase refreshments or use facilities. However, it's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer rides, as services might be intermittent.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and approximately 340 meters of elevation gain over 14.5 km, this route might be challenging for very young children or families new to touring cycling. Families with older, experienced cycling children might find it suitable. For a potentially easier family option, consider exploring the 'Ecopista - Circuito Pedonal de Lousada' which is a flatter, suburban route also suitable for cycling.
